data.description:
## Context / Problem
Discovered from 80af. `rebar transition <id> in_progress closed` (with verify.require_completion_verification_for_close=true) does its work in TWO steps against the git tree — verify, then sign — reading HEAD INDEPENDENTLY each time. A commit landing in the verify→sign window splits them; rebar correctly refuses to attest a tree it didn't verify, but CLOSES THE TICKET ANYWAY without a completion attestation, and its suggested remedy ("re-close to certify") is a no-op because the ticket is already closed. Full report (evidence, repro, analysis): ~/.claude/jobs/e9e3a20d/tmp/rebar-completion-race-bug-report.md

## Evidence (observed)
Closing 4030-df44-540f-4ec0 from a worktree another agent was committing into:
  "Warning: closed ... WITHOUT a completion signature — the code drifted between verify (5d2b47f) and sign (70ca791); not attesting stale state. Re-close to certify against the current tree."
- 5d2b47f = the ticket's real merged work (verifier checked this).
- 70ca791 = an UNRELATED in-flight commit from another agent in the shared worktree (signer saw this).
Post: ticket closed, attestation kinds = ['plan-review'] (NO 'completion'). The suggested re-close is a no-op (already closed). Only "recovery" is --force-close (itself the 'did not validate' signal) or fabricating a manifest via `rebar sign` — not acceptable.

## Root cause (hypothesis)
TOCTOU in transition_close.py: verify_completion(ref="HEAD") resolves HEAD→A; the pre-sign drift guard reads head_sha() again→B; A!=B → refuse sign; the close transition proceeds regardless (verify→close→sign ordering). It treats "the working tree" as an ambient global instead of pinning one revision for the whole verify+sign+close unit. Note: 80af's --ref fix only made the NON-default (--ref) path coherent (verify and sign both pin the ref); the DEFAULT (no --ref) path STILL reads HEAD twice and still splits.

## Expected
Either (a) pin ONE revision for the whole verify+sign+close unit (verify+sign+close all bind that rev; a concurrent commit can't split them) — the attestation binds verified-at-sha:A which is immutable and WAS verified, so an unrelated HEAD move to B should not invalidate it (same 'unrelated movement must not invalidate' principle as 790c's plan-review fix); OR (b) on drift, do NOT close — abort and leave the ticket in_progress so the operator re-runs the close (the plan-review gate's retryable posture). Plus a first-class non-force recovery (e.g. `rebar reverify-completion <id>`, analogous to sign-review/resign_plan_review) for an already-closed-but-unsigned ticket. Immediate 80af-based workaround exists: reopen → transition in_progress closed --ref <verified-sha> re-verifies+signs the real commit coherently.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A completion close whose HEAD moves to an UNRELATED commit between verify and sign does NOT produce a closed-without-signature ticket — either it pins the verified rev and signs it, or it aborts the close and leaves the ticket in_progress (retryable). Verified by a test that moves HEAD in the verify→sign window.
- [ ] There is a non-`--force`, non-fabrication recovery to obtain the completion attestation for a closed-but-unsigned ticket (or the close never lands unsigned on a benign race in the first place).
- [ ] The residual unsigned-close message (if any) names an ACTUALLY-WORKING recovery, not a no-op re-close.
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green.

## Possibly related (from the same report; separate triage)
- PlanReviewGenerationChanged raised with IDENTICAL before==after generation hashes (~19x); blind re-run signs. (Distinct from 790c's related-material case.)
- review-code silent-empty output when backgrounded (exits 0 with empty result).
data.title: completion close: verify->sign TOCTOU race closes a ticket permanently unsigned, with no non-force recovery
